items.title: Royal commission recommends national firearms agreement, buyback scheme

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has released its interim report, making 14 recommendations.

articles.title: Government to implement all national recommendations of antisemitism interim report

children.content: The antisemitism royal commission releases its interim report, recommending an urgent review of Joint Counter Terrorism teams and greater police presence at high-risk Jewish events.  Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese speaks at a press conference

items.title: From cereal to clothing, see how the price of everyday items has changed

children.content: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

image.altText: A group of everyday items; coffee, light bulb, lemon with chart lines

items.title: Concerns for Sharon's survival as police confirm missing 5yo is non-verbal

children.content: Northern Territory Police Commissioner Martin Dole says he is still adamant there are people who have knowledge of the whereabouts of Jefferson Lewis, the man at the centre of a search for missing Alice Springs five-year-old Sharon Granites.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: Coalition helps clear the way for One Nation in Farrer

children.content: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

image.altText: Politician Pauline Hanson gestures to the camera

items.title: Hegseth says Iran war is 'not a quagmire' as cost of conflict revealed

children.content: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has defended the war against Iran in his first testimony to Congress since it started, instead attacking Democrats as "feckless". Follow live.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth with puckered lips, mid-speech, holding up two fingers on his left hand while sitting in congress.

items.title: Israel’s massive strike was opposite of surgical, Lebanon deputy PM says

children.content: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

items.title: Fuel shock pushes civil contractors to cut jobs and pause works

children.content: Civil construction firms warn surging diesel and material costs driven by the global oil crisis will force lay-offs and project delays, with nearly a third considering winding down.

image.altText: Diesel is pumped into a black ute

items.title: 'It's a safety issue': Construction industry faces gambling crisis

children.content: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

image.altText: Men in high-viz and hard hats stand around building supplied chatting.

items.title: Has the world’s most powerful cartel met its end?

children.content: A United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for 66 years.

articles.title: ASX falls as price of oil surges to more than $US120 a barrel

children.content: The Australian share market has opened lower in morning trade as oil prices surge. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

articles.title: Renewables and batteries drive down fossil fuel use despite record electricity demand

children.content: A hot summer saw Australians use record amounts of electricity, but the growing share of renewable energy continued to push fossil fuels out of the grid, driving gas generation to its lowest level in 25 years.

items.title: Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k to MP he sexually harassed and vilified

children.content: The former One Nation MP has been found to have vilified openly gay politician Alex Greenwich in the second case centring around a dispute between the pair.

image.altText: a composite image of Alex Greenwich and Mark Latham

items.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother to remain in custody

children.content: The mother of former police officer Beau Lamarre-Condon will spend at least two weeks in custody after she was charged over an alleged attempt to influence a key witness to change their evidence in his upcoming murder trial.

items.title: Portrait of Jacob Collins wins Archibald Packing Room Prize

items.title: ASIC drops case against Liberty Bell Bay smelter as buyer sought

children.content: It was alleged Liberty Bell Bay's former owner, British billionaire Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance, had failed to lodge financial returns for numerous years. The company then went into administration, and ASIC has dropped its legal case.

items.title: Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraiser and to catch Florida jet

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

items.title: 'Waiting for me to die': Pension stripped from man with disability

children.content: Ray Noakes was put on the disability pension after he was deemed unable to work. Then he was put on Jobseeker, which he lost because he could not work. Now he wants compensation.
